behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Unit could check a parser/comparator, but this behavior is about model-produced signature correctness, not only local logic. Winner then vs System -> no OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Counterbalance System vs Agentic -> signature correctness depends on real LLM output content, so mocks can hide failures. Agentic vs Workflow -> only one model judgment is required, no required multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation for several interface-constrained prompts and assert the produced function signature (name/params/types/return) matches expected interface exactly.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> failure is at module handoff (orchestrator + test-result provider + attempt bookkeeping), so Integration. Winner vs System -> no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because text is about control flow ('executes attempts in order', 'returns immediately') and can be validated with deterministic pass/fail stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stub generator and stub test executor that fails then passes; assert call order is 1..n and loop stops immediately at first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> behavior is not single-function logic; it's loop orchestration plus model outputs. Winner vs System -> even with real infra, core risk is model decision quality across attempts. Counterbalance System vs Agentic -> needs real LLM outputs. Agentic vs Workflow -> explicitly requires iterative live loop with multiple model decisions in sequence until pass, so Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full live loop on a known prompt where first attempt often fails; require that within <=3 attempts at least one run reaches passing tests, and record per-attempt transitions.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> if this were only string formatting it could be Unit, but failure commonly occurs at handoff from test runner results into prompt builder; Integration wins. Winner vs System -> behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because assertion is prompt payload content before model inference, disqualified by 'includes failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt'.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic failing test results from executor, run one failed attempt then inspect next prompt payload; assert failed test identifiers and failure messages are embedded.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> cross-stage behavior, not local logic. Winner vs System -> no special OS/network boundary is the key risk. Counterbalance System vs Agentic -> depends on real LLM response to failure context. Agentic vs Workflow -> requires at least two model decisions (initial + refinement) with context carryover, so Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 with failing tests, build refinement context, run attempt 2 live, and assert attempt-2 code differs materially and targets prior failure signals (not identical resubmission).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> combines loop control, attempt storage, and error shaping across modules; Integration. Winner vs System -> no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because this behavior is deterministic termination/bookkeeping ('stops after 3 failed attempts', 'returns structured error') and can be forced with stubbed failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic stubs that always fail tests; assert exactly 3 attempts executed, service terminates, and error object contains all 3 attempts with corresponding test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> explicitly 'full ... wiring ... end to end' across modules, so Integration. Winner vs System -> behavior explicitly says 'non-agentic' and 'deterministic stubbed model outputs'; no required real OS/network/subprocess boundary is stated. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by explicit text 'non-agentic' and 'stubbed model outputs'.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with stubbed generator outputs and deterministic test executor responses; verify generate->test->bookkeeping transitions and final status are correct.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> multi-stage loop, not local logic. Winner vs System -> real subprocess/test runner may exist, but key failure boundary is model-driven solving across stages. Counterbalance System vs Agentic -> requires real LLM output quality. Agentic vs Workflow -> explicitly multi-step agentic chain ('generate, test, refine, retest') with sequential model decisions; Workflow wins.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live pipeline on a curated hard prompt with known first-attempt failure; assert first attempt fails and a later attempt within budget passes, preserving full step-by-step trace.
